sectionDescription: Introduce basic greetings, simple self-introduction, classroom words, and short spoken phrases. Focus on listening, recognition, and confidence.

levels.levelDescription: Learn how to greet people and say goodbye politely.

questions.text: Which phrase is used to say goodbye?

levels.levelDescription: Recognize basic alphabet sounds and simple phonics.

questions.text: Which word starts with the sound /k/?
Match the sound to the word.

levels.levelDescription: Introduce yourself using simple sentences.

questions.text: What does 'Nice to meet you' mean?

levels.levelDescription: Learn words for people and objects in the classroom.

questions.text: Match the word with its meaning.

levels.levelDescription: Practice simple phrases and responses through repetition.

questions.text: What is the best reply to 'How are you?'
True or False: 'Hello' and 'Goodbye' have the same meaning.

sectionDescription: Learners talk about familiar things such as family, toys, colors, numbers, and personal belongings using very simple sentences.

levels.levelDescription: Learn basic family words and talk about family members.
Name common toys and say what you have.
Recognize and use basic color words.
Count from one to ten and use numbers with nouns.
Use 'this is' to introduce people and objects near you.

sectionDescription: Learners talk about daily activities, routines, time, and simple actions using the present simple tense.

levels.levelDescription: Learn common daily action verbs such as eat, sleep, play, and go.
Describe simple daily routines using 'I' statements.

options: I go to school every day.
questions.correctAnswer: I go to school every day.
wake up go to school sleep

levels.levelDescription: Use basic time expressions such as morning, afternoon, and night.

questions.text: Which one is a time of day?

levels.levelDescription: Talk about habits using present simple with adverbs like always and usually.

questions.text: I ___ brush my teeth in the morning.

levels.levelDescription: Express likes using simple verbs and nouns.

questions.text: Match the sentence with the meaning.

sectionDescription: Learners talk about school objects, subjects, classroom actions, and simple rules using present simple sentences.

levels.levelDescription: Learn common classroom objects such as book, pen, desk, and bag.

questions.text: Which one is a school object?
Match the object with its use.

levels.levelDescription: Use simple sentences to describe what happens in the classroom.
Identify basic school subjects and talk about what you study.

questions.text: Which one is a school subject?
True or False: Math is a school subject.

levels.levelDescription: Learn simple classroom rules using positive statements.

questions.text: Which sentence shows a classroom rule?

levels.levelDescription: Talk about favorite school subjects using simple present sentences.

questions.text: Match the subject with the sentence.
True or False: Favorite means you like it the most.

sectionDescription: Learners talk about family members, relationships, and simple descriptions using the present simple tense.

levels.levelDescription: Learn basic family words such as mother, father, brother, and sister.

questions.text: Which one is a family member?
Match the word with the meaning.

levels.levelDescription: Use 'he' and 'she' to talk about family members.

questions.text: True or False: We use 'he' for a girl.

levels.levelDescription: Talk about how many people are in your family.

questions.text: I have ___ people in my family.

options: I have five people in my family.
I people five have my family.
questions.correctAnswer: I have five people in my family.

questions.text: Put the family members in order.

levels.levelDescription: Describe family members using simple adjectives.
Make short statements about your family using simple present sentences.

questions.text: True or False: 'Parents' means mother and father.

sectionDescription: Learners talk about their home, rooms, furniture, and simple activities at home using present simple sentences.

levels.levelDescription: Learn the names of common rooms such as bedroom, kitchen, and living room.

questions.text: Match the room with its use.

levels.levelDescription: Identify common furniture items found at home.
Talk about simple activities you do at home.

questions.text: True or False: We cook food at home.

levels.levelDescription: Describe your home using simple adjectives.

questions.text: Match the word with its meaning.

levels.levelDescription: Talk about your favorite room using simple present sentences.

options: The room you like the most
questions.correctAnswer: The room you like the most

pattern.baseSentence: My favorite room is the kitchen.
pattern.exampleSentence: My favorite room is the bedroom.
pattern.questionSentence: My favorite room ___ the living room.

questions.text: True or False: Favorite means you like it the most.

sectionDescription: Learners talk about common food, drinks, likes, and simple eating habits using present simple sentences.

levels.levelDescription: Learn the names of common foods such as rice, bread, and noodles.

questions.text: Match the food with the group.

levels.levelDescription: Identify common drinks and talk about what you drink.

questions.text: True or False: Milk is a drink.

levels.levelDescription: Express likes and dislikes about food and drinks.

questions.text: True or False: 'I like rice' means you enjoy rice.

levels.levelDescription: Learn meal names such as breakfast, lunch, and dinner.

options: The first meal of the day
The last meal of the day
questions.correctAnswer: The first meal of the day

levels.levelDescription: Talk about healthy food using simple sentences.

questions.text: True or False: Vegetables are healthy.

sectionDescription: Learners talk about basic weather conditions and choose suitable clothes using simple present sentences.

levels.levelDescription: Learn basic weather words such as sunny, rainy, and cold.

questions.text: True or False: Rainy means there is rain.

levels.levelDescription: Talk about temperature using simple adjectives.
Learn common clothing items such as shirt, pants, and jacket.

questions.text: Match the clothes with the body part.

levels.levelDescription: Choose suitable clothes for different weather.

questions.text: What do you wear when it is cold?
Which clothes are good for hot weather?
It is rainy, so I wear a ___.

options: I wear a jacket when it is cold.
I wear when cold it is jacket.
questions.correctAnswer: I wear a jacket when it is cold.

questions.text: True or False: We wear a raincoat when it is rainy.

levels.levelDescription: Talk about your favorite weather using simple present sentences.

options: The weather you like the most
questions.correctAnswer: The weather you like the most

questions.text: True or False: Favorite means you like it the most.

sectionDescription: Learners identify common places and describe their location using simple sentences and prepositions.

levels.levelDescription: Learn names of common places such as school, park, and hospital.

questions.text: True or False: A park is a place.

levels.levelDescription: Describe distance using simple adjectives.
Use basic prepositions to describe location.

questions.text: True or False: We use 'at' for places.

levels.levelDescription: Talk about your favorite place using simple present sentences.

options: The place you like the most
questions.correctAnswer: The place you like the most

questions.text: True or False: A hospital is a place.

levels.levelDescription: Ask and answer simple questions about places.

questions.text: True or False: We use 'Where' to ask about places.

sectionDescription: Learners describe daily activities using simple present tense and basic time expressions.

levels.levelDescription: Learn common daily actions such as wake up, eat, study, and sleep.

questions.text: Which one is a daily action?

options: I go to school every day.
I goes to school every day.
I going to school every day.
questions.correctAnswer: I go to school every day.

questions.text: True or False: 'Sleep' is a daily activity.

levels.levelDescription: Talk about activities you do in the morning.

questions.text: Which activity is done in the morning?

levels.levelDescription: Describe what you do in the afternoon and evening.

options: She does homework in the evening.
She do homework in the evening.
She doing homework in the evening.
questions.correctAnswer: She does homework in the evening.

questions.text: Which activity is usually in the evening?
True or False: People usually sleep in the afternoon.

levels.levelDescription: Use simple time words such as morning, afternoon, evening, and night.

questions.text: True or False: 'Night' is after evening.

levels.levelDescription: Describe your daily routine using simple present tense.

questions.text: Which sentence talks about a daily routine?
Match the time with the activity.
True or False: Daily routines use the simple present tense.
